subject: Create runtime setup for the script
commit_message:
Create runtime setup for the script

Change-Id: I1a1207173b065c3b88b4b62e134c2fda33e0e6ec
Issue-ID: INT-1642
Signed-off-by: Bartek Grzybowski <b.grzybowski@partner.samsung.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/integration-master-python-lint/199/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/integration-master-python-lint/199

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

see my comments in the JIRA
i think it is better to create the requirement here and build the setup (it is currently done at le docker level)

Patch Set 2:

If you could also add a final status explicitely telling the test is PASS or FAIL (return code or log) it would be helpful for the CI

Patch Set 2:

This commit is only adding more Python native way of defining the runtime environment for the script - previously it was required to install packages (kubernetes and colorama) currently by hand, now tox takes care of it by creating virtualenv.

Hence I'm not sure i understand the context of "final status" "PASS or FAIL"?

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> This commit is only adding more Python native way of defining the runtime environment for the script - previously it was required to install packages (kubernetes and colorama) currently by hand, now tox takes care of it by creating virtualenv.
> 
> Hence I'm not sure i understand the context of "final status" "PASS or FAIL"?

agree and inline with you, it is better to do that here (rather at the xtesting level)
As it was not done here I added the requirements in the xtesting docker file including this test
https://git.onap.org/integration/xtesting/tree/infra-healthcheck/requirements.txt
xtesting is ~ virtual env (it is a docker) but it is better when python packages are properly packaged earlier

the question regarding the success criteria is more dealing wit the test itself
if you run this script, you got logs
but at the end you do not know if the test is PASS or FAIL, you have to compare the number of ingress and number of port I think
to include it in CI, we should get a clear statement that could be a log "Test PASS" or the return code of the test (return 0 if PASS)
but as far as I remember we do not have such things, so we can execute the test and see the logs. The success criteria has not been set.
Change has been successfully rebased and submitted as ee1fc1603d774676e80bcef6c96946efa87965ce by Morgan Richomme

> but as far as I remember we do not have such things, so we can execute the test and see the logs. The success criteria has not been set.

Ok thanks for clarification, I didn't get the part concerning requirements setup in xtesting. And yes also think this should belong to the script directory/repo.

As for the success criteria - maybe you should raise a ticket for it and ping Lucjan who knows this code best? Lucjan would you like to provide such feature?
